lessons.text: Cut the tangled tree of attachment at its root.
lessons.rationale: The upside-down cosmic tree of worldly existence, felled with the strong axe of non-attachment.

lessons.text: Take refuge in the source from which none return.
lessons.rationale: Seek the primeval Purusha, the eternal goal beyond rebirth.

lessons.text: Rise above pride, delusion, and the pull of opposites.
lessons.rationale: The undeluded conquer attachment and the pairs of pleasure and pain to reach the eternal.

lessons.text: Know yourself as an eternal fragment of the divine.
lessons.rationale: A portion of the Lord becomes the living soul that draws the senses to itself.

lessons.text: Open the eye of knowledge to see the Self.
lessons.rationale: The deluded miss the indwelling Self; those with the eye of knowledge behold Him.

lessons.text: Recognize one divine light in sun, moon, and fire.
lessons.rationale: The one energy illumines, nourishes, and digests through all of nature.

lessons.text: Trace your memory and knowledge to their source within.
lessons.rationale: The divine seated in the heart is the origin of memory and knowledge.

lessons.text: Look beyond the changing and unchanging to the supreme.
lessons.rationale: The Supreme Purusha transcends both the perishable and the imperishable.

lessons.text: Know the supreme Self and your duty fulfills itself.
lessons.rationale: Knowing the highest Purusha makes one wise and accomplishes all duties.

skipped.reason: Purely metaphysical description of the supreme abode beyond sun, moon, and fire; no distinct practical takeaway.
